chief_complaint: A 70-year-old Chinese woman presented with an acute stroke and incidental finding of a likely large meningioma with surrounding vasogenic edema.
history: The patient was clinically stable and undergoing inpatient rehabilitation in the general ward setting. She was started on a tapering dose of dexamethasone for the meningioma. Shortly after, lactic acidosis was discovered on the basis of low bicarbonate.
exam: The patient remained asymptomatic and clinically well throughout this period.
labs: Type B lactic acidosis, low bicarbonate, dexamethasone was the likely cause of the elevated lactate.
differentials: Type B lactic acidosis due to other medications
Type A lactic acidosis due to tissue hypoperfusion
correct_ranking: Type B lactic acidosis due to other medications
Type A lactic acidosis due to tissue hypoperfusion
